title: baccharis tridentata var. subopposita
intro: Baccharis tridentata var. subopposita, also known as Coyote Mint, is a plant species within the Baccharis genus, native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, Coyote Mint has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent qualities, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Baccharis tridentata var. subopposita include its potential to alleviate digestive issues, soothe skin irritations, and reduce fever. Coyote Mint is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties in a variety of forms.
